title: CorpusFilterRules
description: Governed ruleset defining what is stripped from document text before topic modeling. Each rule identifies a token class that describes the artifact (provenance, administration, identity) rather than the content. Stripped tokens are routed to the semantic layer they actually belong to, not discarded.

corpus_domain.description: Domain this ruleset is designed for, e.g. academic_papers, operational_artifacts, legal_documents

strip_class.description: The class of token being stripped. Classes ending in _INDIVIDUAL mark entities (not topics). All others mark administrative or structural noise.

reason.description: Human-readable rationale. Must answer: why does this class not belong in topic space?

semantic_layer.description: Where stripped tokens belong instead. provenance=citation/lineage layer, identity=CSKG individual node, administrative=catalog metadata, discard=no semantic value anywhere.

action.description: strip=remove entirely; route_to_layer=remove from content but record in semantic_layer; normalize_then_strip=clean and record before removing.

patterns.description: Exact terms, prefix patterns, or regex strings to match. Evaluated case-insensitively.

regex_patterns.description: Full regex patterns for more complex matching (e.g. year detection, arXiv IDs).
